task_completion.description: Create rectangles for each product and label them with the product names.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Diagrams.net is open and contains rectangular shapes labeled with the product names: FiberCrunch, ChocoBlast, SugarDust, HeartyOats, and BudgetBag.

task_completion.description: Scale the product rectangles correctly based on the 10px = 1 inch rule.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The rectangles should have varying heights reflecting their real-world sizes (relative to the 720px tall fixture): BudgetBag is the tallest (140px), followed by FiberCrunch (120px), HeartyOats (110px), and ChocoBlast/SugarDust are the shortest (100px). Check the geometry/size panel in Diagrams.net if visible.

task_completion.description: Position kids' items on the bottom shelf (0-20 inches from bottom).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The rectangles labeled 'ChocoBlast' and 'SugarDust' are placed at the very bottom of the fixture (within the lowest 200px of the 720px fixture).

task_completion.description: Position high-margin items at eye level (50-60 inches from bottom).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The rectangles labeled 'FiberCrunch' and 'HeartyOats' are placed in the upper-middle section of the fixture (between 500px and 600px from the bottom).

task_completion.description: Position value/bulk items on the top shelf.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The rectangle(s) labeled 'BudgetBag' are placed at the very top of the fixture (above the eye-level items, >600px from the bottom).

task_completion.description: Identify the lowest margin product with a 'Star' shape labeled 'SALE'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A star shape containing the text 'SALE' is placed on or immediately next to the 'BudgetBag' product (which is the lowest margin item).

task_completion.description: Export the completed planogram to a PDF file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent navigates to File -> Export as -> PDF in Diagrams.net, or a file manager/terminal shows 'planogram_export.pdf' successfully created in the ~/Diagrams/ directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the Diagrams.net GUI to create the planogram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent actively using the Diagrams.net (draw.io) graphical interface to drag, drop, resize, and label shapes.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the GUI by editing the .drawio XML file directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editor (like nano, vim, or gedit) is shown open with the raw XML code of the planogram_template.drawio file.

integrity.description: The agent exported the PDF using the application's built-in export functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Diagrams.net export dialog is visible in the screenshots, rather than the agent using a command-line conversion utility.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Fixture dimensions: 480px W x 720px H. Scale: 10px = 1 inch. Product Heights: BudgetBag (140px), FiberCrunch (120px), HeartyOats (110px), ChocoBlast (100px), SugarDust (100px). Target Zones (measured from bottom): Bottom shelf = 0-200px (ChocoBlast, SugarDust). Eye level = 500-600px (FiberCrunch, HeartyOats). Top shelf = >600px (BudgetBag). Lowest Margin Product: BudgetBag ($0.50 margin) - this is the ONLY product that should receive the 'SALE' star. Minimum required facings (shapes) based on CSV: 11 total (2 FiberCrunch, 3 ChocoBlast, 3 SugarDust, 2 HeartyOats, 1 BudgetBag).
